title: The UK and International Institutions
overview: The United Kingdom works closely with other nations to promote peace, security, and prosperity. Through international organisations, Britain plays an active role in solving global problems, supporting development, and protecting human rights.

study_sections.heading: The United Nations (UN)
study_sections.content: The United Nations was founded in 1945 to maintain peace after WWII. The UK is a founding member and one of five permanent members of the UN Security Council. British peacekeepers and aid workers serve in UN programmes worldwide.

study_sections.heading: The Commonwealth
study_sections.content: The Commonwealth of Nations includes 56 independent countries. The UK monarch is the symbolic Head. Members work together to promote democracy, development, and human rights through cooperation and friendship.

study_sections.heading: NATO
study_sections.content: NATO was established in 1949 for collective defence—an attack on one is an attack on all. The UK is a founding member and contributes to peacekeeping and security missions.

study_sections.heading: World Trade Organization
study_sections.content: The WTO helps nations trade fairly and settle disputes. The UK works to ensure open and fair global trade.

study_sections.heading: The Council of Europe
study_sections.content: Created in 1949 to protect human rights in Europe. The European Convention on Human Rights inspired the UK Human Rights Act 1998.

study_sections.heading: UK Global Role
study_sections.content: Britain supports global stability through diplomacy, development aid, and peacekeeping via the Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office (FCDO).

key_facts: UK is permanent UN Security Council member
Five permanent Security Council members: China, France, Russia, UK, USA
UK Monarch is Head of Commonwealth
NATO principle: an attack on one is an attack on all
European Convention on Human Rights protects rights
Human Rights Act 1998 includes Convention rights
FCDO manages foreign policy and aid
BBC World Service provides international news
British Council promotes education and culture overseas
memory_hook: UK global connections: UN (peace 1945), Commonwealth (56 family members), NATO (defense 1949), WTO (fair trade), Council of Europe (rights 1949), FCDO (diplomacy). Like a friendship network—UN for resolving fights, Commonwealth for family reunions, NATO for protection, WTO for sharing resources, Council for agreeing on values.

questions.prompt: When was the United Nations founded?
questions.explanation: The UN was established in 1945 after WWII.

questions.prompt: How many permanent members does the UN Security Council have?
questions.explanation: The UK is one of five permanent members.

questions.prompt: How many countries are Commonwealth members?
Who is Head of the Commonwealth?
questions.explanation: The UK Monarch is symbolic Head.

options: Mutual defence—attack on one is attack on all

flashcards: Attack on one is attack on all
When was Council of Europe created?
